title: 10 best herbal creams for upset stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with upset stomach because of its unique properties. The herb glycyrrhizin, found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and soothing qualities that can calm digestive issues such as nausea, bloating, and cramps. When applied topically to the skin, the cream absorbs into the bloodstream, providing relief from stomach discomfort. Additionally, licorice creams contain antacids that help neutralize stomach acid, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut environment. This natural remedy provides effective relief from upset stomachs without harsh chemicals or additives.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which helps to calm digestive irritation and reduce inflammation in the gut, providing relief from stomach discomfort.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce oxidative stress caused by digestive issues.
Triterpenoids: These molecules have been shown to have a direct effect on the gut microbiome, promoting the growth of beneficial bacteria and improving digestion, absorption, and overall gut health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the stomach.
Barks: The barks contain triterpenoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease digestive discomfort in patients with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil reaches a temperature of 70°c to 80°c.
Allow the mixture to cool and then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Add 2% w/w beeswax and 0.5% w/w vitamin e oil as preservatives, and mix well before pouring the cream into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams help with upset stomach because they contain a soothing and calming compound that alleviates digestive discomfort. When applied topically, chamomile cream can ease spasms in the muscles of the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile also help to calm irritation and reduce nausea, providing relief from symptoms associated with upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramping, spasms, and discomfort associated with upset stomach and IBS.

Sotheoing properties: Chamomile's soothing compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce stress, anxiety, and tension that can exacerbate upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots help soothe and calm digestive issues, making them an ideal ingredient for soothing upset stomach creams.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, a flavonoid that calms inflammation and relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of grated beeswax with 6 tablespoons of coconut oil and heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of the ground chamomile powder to the melted beeswax-coconut oil mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool at room temperature until it reaches a creamy consistency, taking approximately 30-40 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The creams contain extracts of ginger root, which have been used for centuries to calm nausea and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating. When applied topically, the cream helps to reduce inflammation in the gut, relieving discomfort and cramping associated with upset stomach. Additionally, ginger's natural antimicrobial properties can help to combat bad bacteria in the digestive system, promoting a healthy balance and further reducing stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea, bloating, and cramps associated with upset stomach.

Digestive enzymes stimulant: Ginger's digestive enzymes stimulate the release of stomach acids and digestive enzymes, which helps break down food more efficiently and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating.

Antioxidant and anti-spasmodic properties: Ginger's antioxidant and anti-spasmodic properties help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excess acid production and reduce muscle spasms that can exacerbate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues.
Roots: Ginger roots contain a high concentration of volatile oils that are responsible for its medicinal properties, including reducing nausea and stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh ginger rhizomes measuring one ounce per recipe batch.
Grind the ginger using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle into fine powder.
Mix the ground ginger with coconut oil in a 1:4 ratio by weight stirring well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes slowly.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into glass jars cooling to room temperature fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural calming properties. The menthol in peppermint relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and spasms that can cause discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe the gut lining, decreasing irritation and pain. This combination of actions eases digestive distress, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea associated with an upset stomach. As a result, peppermint creams provide rapid and effective relief for those struggling with indigestion and other stomach issues.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms such as bloating and cramps.

Peppermint Oil: The active compounds in peppermint oil, including menthone and limonene, have been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and pain associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other stomach issues.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint creams, such as camphor and eucalyptol, have natural antibacterial properties that help to eliminate bad bacteria from the gut, promoting a healthy digestive environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have a soothing and calming effect, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant contain menthol, which helps to relax the muscles and calm digestive issues such as cramps and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried peppermint leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered peppermint with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl to create an infusion.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes, allowing the peppermint's active compounds to dissolve into the water.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.

Combine the peppermint infusion with 50g of beeswax pellets and 25ml of coconut oil in a small saucepan, heating until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60-70°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth, spreadable cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with upset stomach because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract and calm irritated tissues. The cream's active ingredients, such as fenchone and anethole, work to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle spasms in the stomach, alleviating symptoms like bloating, cramps, and gas. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to release trapped gases and ease digestion, providing quick relief from upset stomach discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel cream's carminative property helps to relieve gas, bloating, and discomfort by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive tract.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compound anethole in fennel cream reduces inflammation in the gut lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as cramps, diarrhea, and constipation.

Digestive enzyme stimulant property: Fennel cream's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes helps to improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, indigestion, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, making them ideal for relieving upset stomach symptoms.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have been traditionally used to aid digestion and relieve bloating, gas, and indigestion, making them a popular choice for medicinal creams for upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry foeniculum vulgare seeds for one hour in a warm oven set at 150°f.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix two tablespoons of the powdered foeniculum vulgare with four tablespoons of sweet almond oil and three tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes or until the beeswax has melted, stirring occasionally.
Strain the cream into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator at 39°f for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with upset stomach because of its unique properties that calm the digestive system. The cream's active ingredient, Cinnamomum verum, contains cinnamic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the cinnamon also relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to pass through more smoothly and reducing cramps and discomfort. As a result, Ceylon cinnamon creams provide fast and effective relief from upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ceylon cinnamon creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), bloating, and cramps.
Carminative properties: The cream's carminative properties help to release trapped gas and reduce spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from stomach discomfort, bloating, and flatulence.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon creams have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and fungi in the gut, reducing symptoms of indigestion, diarrhea, and stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the Ceylon cinnamon plant, which contains high concentrations of cinnamaldehyde, a compound responsible for its anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.

Barks: The bark of the Ceylon cinnamon plant has been used in traditional medicine to soothe digestive issues, including upset stomachs, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate spasms.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 100g of beeswax pellets in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Add 50ml of sweet almond oil and 25ml of coconut oil to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and heat it gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with upset stomach because of its unique blend of natural ingredients that soothe and calm the digestive system. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce irritation and swelling in the gut, while its antispasmodic agents ease cramps and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's bitter compounds stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, helping to regulate bowel function and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach, including bloating, gas, and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Bitter compounds: Dandelion cream contains bitter compounds like sesquiterpenes, which stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and nausea by increasing digestive juices and improving gut motility.

Anti-inflammatory agents: The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastritis, and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals and harmful bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing symptoms of stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with upset stomach.
Barks: The barks contain a compound called taraxasterol, which has been shown to have anti-ulcer properties, helping to protect the stomach lining from damage.
remedy_recipe: Gather the necessary ingredients including dried taraxacum officinale flowers one ounce per recipe.
Measure and mix together a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil one cup with two ounces of dried herbs.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 48 hours to allow for infusion and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Add an emulsifying agent such as beeswax one tablespoon per recipe and whip until smooth and creamy preparation takes approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with upset stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The herb's natural ingredients, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping, making it a popular remedy for irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive issues. By reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation in the stomach muscles, St John's Wort creams provide fast relief from bloating, gas, and nausea, helping to restore digestive balance and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: St John's Wort creams contain hyperforin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to upset stomach and nausea.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in St John's Wort creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining and exacerbate digestive issues.

Soothing and calming properties: The herbal extracts in St John's Wort creams have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help ease symptoms like cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are used to create soothing and calming creams that alleviate digestive discomfort.
Flowers: With their high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, flowers help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps associated with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers from a trusted source or purchase dried herb from a reputable supplier.
Dry the fresh herb in a warm oven at 60°c for 12 hours or air-dry it for 24 hours to preserve its potency.
Combine 50g of dried hypericum perforatum with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids, and add 20g of beeswax to thicken the liquid.
Melt the mixture over low heat in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water for 30 minutes until it reaches a consistency suitable for use as a topical cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with upset stomach because of its unique composition. The main active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the digestive system, promoting a healthy gut environment and reducing the risk of irritation and infection. This natural remedy provides fast and effective relief for upset stomachs without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: The active compound in turmeric, curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.

Cicloartenol: A natural constituent found in turmeric, cicloartenol exhibits antioxidant properties that can help protect the gut lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of ulcers and irritation.

Gingerols: Ginger extracts present in turmeric creams have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract, reduce nausea and vomiting, and alleviate abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems store nutrients and have anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe digestive issues.
Roots: The main storage organ of the plant, roots contain bioactive compounds like curcuminoids that aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric rhizomes carefully selecting those with vibrant yellow coloration for maximum curcumin content.
Dry the harvested turmeric rhizomes in a warm well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight for 7 days.
Grind the dried turmeric powder into a fine consistency using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine one part ground turmeric with two parts coconut oil and one part beeswax in a double boiler whipping until smooth for 10 minutes.
Pour the herbal curcuma longa cream mixture into sterilized jars sealing tightly and allowing it to cool at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in wormwood, such as sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), acid reflux, and other gastrointestinal disorders. By reducing inflammation, wormwood creams help to alleviate cramping, bloating, and discomfort associated with upset stomachs, providing relief from digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Wormwood's astringent compounds help to reduce inflammation and slow down digestion, which can provide relief from diarrhea and other gastrointestinal issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe irritated mucous membranes in the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with gastritis and ulcers.

Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial compounds can help to kill off harmful bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to stomach upset, reducing the risk of infection and promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wormwood plant are rich in tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues.
Barks: The barks of the wormwood plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm upset stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves measure out 20 grams of dried leaves.
Combine the dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes until it reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10 grams of beeswax.
Pour the infused oil into a heat-proof container and melt the beeswax within 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with upset stomach because of its calming and soothing properties. The herb, a member of the mint family, contains compounds that can help ease digestive issues by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract. When applied topically as a cream, sweet marjoram's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects can provide fast relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with an upset stomach, allowing for faster recovery and improved digestion.

Carminative property: The carminative property of marjoram helps to reduce flatulence and bloating by preventing the formation of gas in the digestive system, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and dyspepsia.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce stomach pain.
Seeds: Containing oil-rich compounds, seeds provide a natural lubricant that can ease stomach cramps and spasms, providing relief from upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried origanum majorana leaves and oil carrier base at room temperature.
Combine 20 grams of dried origanum majorana leaves with 100 milliliters of boiling water to create a tincture, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding solids and retaining liquid extract.
Mix 20 grams of petroleum jelly with 30 milliliters of the origanum majorana liquid extract, stirring until fully incorporated and smooth in texture.
Fill sterilized jars with the origanum majorana cream and seal tightly, allowing to cool and solidify at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with upset stomach because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called echinacea, which soothes the digestive tract and reduces inflammation in the gut. The cream's active ingredients also help to calm the stomach lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with indigestion and bloating. Additionally, the gentle, non-irritating formula of herbal kansas coneflower creams allows for easy absorption into the skin, making it an effective topical treatment for upset stomach relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues such as bloating and cramps.

Antimicrobial agents: Kansas coneflower creams contain antimicrobial agents like terpenoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can contribute to upset stomach and digestive discomfort.

Digestive stimulants: The cream contains alkaloids like esculeoside A, which has been found to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms such as bloating, gas, and indigestion by increasing gut motility and reducing spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the Kansas coneflower plant have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 ounces of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and 10 ounces of coconut oil for infusion.
Combine the dried echinacea angustifolia roots with 4 cups of distilled water in a heat-proof glass container.
Infuse the mixture by heating it at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes, then straining the liquid using a cheesecloth or coffee filter.
Mix 8 ounces of cooled echinacea angustifolia infusion with 6 ounces of beeswax and 10 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before whipping it into a creamy consistency using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The gel inside aloe vera leaves contains powerful compounds that reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, providing relief from symptoms like bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, aloe's mucilages help to protect the stomach lining from further irritation, promoting healing and restoration of a healthy gut environment. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea.

Mucilages: Aloe vera gel is rich in mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the digestive system, providing relief from heartburn, indigestion, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) symptoms.

Antioxidants: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants like vitamins C and E, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the digestive tract, promoting healing and protecting against damage caused by stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and cut them into small pieces to release the gel for extraction.
Combine 100g of aloe vera gel with 20g of dried peppermint leaf in a heat-resistant bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes.
Add 10g of dried ginger powder and stir well before heating the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 20ml of sweet almond oil and 5ml of beeswax, stirring until melted and combined.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow it to solidify fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry creams helps with upset stomach because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. Berberine has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea. remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a bioactive compound found in barberry, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and calm an upset stomach by reducing inflammation and eliminating harmful bacteria.

Chelidonic acid: Chelidonic acid, another key constituent of barberry, has been shown to have anti-gastrointestinal spasmodic effects, helping to relax the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea associated with upset stomach.

Flavonoids and alkaloids: The flavonoids and alkaloids present in barberry creams have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, leading to a healthier gut environment and reduced symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the barberry plant contain berberine, an alkaloid compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the barberry plant have been traditionally used to treat a range of gastrointestinal problems, including stomach upset, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis vulgars roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the washed roots in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours or until brittle.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix 250 grams of powdered root with 125 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 25 millilitres of beeswax in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat stir well and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before filling jars and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with upset stomach because of its calming and soothing properties. The essential oil extracted from the flowers of the lavender plant has natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce nausea. Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, seeds of English lavender aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality l. angustifolia flowers in shaded areas or with machine drying equipment.
Combine 200 grams of dried flowers with 400 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2.
Steep the mixture for four hours under low heat conditions to facilitate efficient extraction of essential oils.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into sterilized containers and discard the solids.
Blend the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of emulsifying wax in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with upset stomach because they contain the herb angelica, which has natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves by cutting them at the base with scissors.
Clean the harvested plant material by rinsing it under cold running water then pat dry with paper towels.
Dry the cleaned plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or air drying for 24 hours.
Combine dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves with coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler until melted at 180°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before sealing.

remedy_parts: Roots: Cornflower roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues due to their ability to soothe and calm inflammation in the gut.
Seeds: The seeds of the cornflower contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried centaurea cyanus flowers 20g, coconut oil 100ml, beeswax 10g and vitamin e oil 5 drops.
Grind the dried centaurea cyanus flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes then remove from heat.
Add the ground centaurea cyanus flowers to the melted coconut oil stirring well for 5 minutes before adding beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Whisk the mixture until smooth and creamy then pour into a sterilized jar cooling and storing at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams helps with upset stomach because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm digestive issues. The cream's rich content of silica, a natural relaxant, helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. Additionally, field horsetail's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate harmful bacteria and yeast from the gut, promoting a healthy balance of flora and reducing digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Field horsetail contains anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach such as bloating and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of field horsetail helps neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the gastrointestinal lining, leading to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Demulcent properties: Field horsetail's mucilage content creates a protective barrier on the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, soothing and calming irritated tissues and providing relief from heartburn, indigestion, and stomach pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in silica, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, providing relief from symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest equisetum arvense plants in the morning after dew has dried, ensuring cleanliness and air-drying them for several days.
Combine 20 grams of dried equisetum arvense herb with 40 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring frequently, until the beeswax has melted and the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before straining through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Allow the cream to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before whipping with a whisk until smooth, then transferring to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with upset stomach because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritated digestive tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The cream's natural ingredients also help to relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating cramps and spasms associated with digestive issues. Additionally, stinging nettle's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation and slow down gastric acid production, further calming the stomach and promoting a sense of relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, carotenoids, and terpenes that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in nettle creams, including alkaloids and glycosides, help relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to upset stomach symptoms like abdominal pain and nausea.

Soothers for irritated mucous membranes: Nettle creams contain soothing agents like mucilages and saponins that calm and protect irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms of upset stomach such as heartburn, acid reflux, and gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm upset stomach symptoms.
Seeds: Stinging nettle seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including upset stomach, due to their carminative properties that help expel gas and ease bloating.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried leaves of urtica dioica, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Combine one cup of dried leaves with two cups of boiling water to create an infusion for 20 minutes.
Strain the infusion and mix it with four ounces of melted coconut oil and one teaspoon of beeswax.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into a sterile container and store it in the refrigerator for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for upset stomach are topical remedies that harness the natural healing properties of herbs to soothe and calm digestive discomfort. These creams, often infused with ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and peppermint, work by reducing inflammation, alleviating cramps, and relaxing the muscles in the abdominal area. For example, creams containing ginger have been shown to effectively alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), allowing individuals to resume their daily activities without discomfort. As a result, people can enjoy life's moments without the distraction of stomach upset, and feel more confident and empowered to take on their day with ease.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat upset stomach with herbal creams is to choose products containing soothing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, or peppermint. Apply the cream topically to the affected area to alleviate discomfort and reduce inflammation. For optimal relief, use creams specifically designed for digestive issues and follow the recommended dosage instructions carefully.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upset stomach that are treated with herbal creams are irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), diarrhea, constipation, and hemorrhoids. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and slippery elm can help soothe irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the digestive tract. These natural remedies can provide relief from symptoms such as pain, itching, and discomfort associated with these conditions.
